desc: Apply two security fixes to security-middleware.js in the Africa Watch backend. FIX 1 — HSTS: In the attachSecurityMiddleware function, inside the security headers app.use block (around line 136-145), add this line after the existing setHeader calls: res.setHeader('Strict-Transport-Security', 'max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload'); FIX 2 — CORS lockdown for unauthenticated public endpoints: The current CORS middleware (lines 147-165) sets CORS headers only when origin matches allowedOrigins — this is already correct. BUT the public read endpoints (GET /counties, GET /timeline, GET /alerts, GET /trends, GET /factchecks, GET /africa/events, GET /africa/countries, GET /search-sources, GET /keywords/trending) are currently returning full data to ANY origin that doesnt send an Origin header (direct curl/server-side requests). Add an explicit CORS rejection for browser requests from unknown origins: in the CORS middleware, after the block that sets headers for allowed origins, add: else if (origin) { return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Origin not allowed' }); } — this blocks browser cross-origin requests from unknown origins while still allowing same-origin and direct API calls (no Origin header). Also add ALLOWED_ORIGINS env var documentation comment near getAllowedOrigins(). Return unified git diff against security-middleware.js only.
instructions: Return a valid unified git diff only. No markdown. No code fences. No explanation. The diff must apply cleanly to C:/Users/shuru/Documents/AIProjects/Kenya-Watch/output/security-middleware.js. The CSP_HEADER constant is at line 9. The attachSecurityMiddleware function starts at line 134. The CORS middleware is lines 150-165. Make minimal targeted changes only.
